sections.paragraph: m. (from the root נוּב) produce, fruit, Mal 1:2. Metaph. נִיב שְׂפָתַיִם “fruit of the lips,” i.e. offerings rendered to God by the lips, thanksgivings, compare καρπὸς χειλέων, Heb 13:15. Isa 57:19, “I create the fruit of the lips;” I cause that they give praise to God. In כתיב there is נוב, comp. Ch. נֹוב fruit.
